mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue expansion and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity that forces equity repricing, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of industrial production declines during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading exceeding 3.5% signals persistent inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of occurrences during early-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic activity that directly boosts corporate earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters with positive GDP growth over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks that delay earnings normalization, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of industrial production declines during post-shock recovery periods.
